title: NIS2 Art.21(2)(j) — Secure emergency communication channel verification

compliance.assurance_rationale: Verifies availability and encryption of emergency communication channels (out-of-band comms, dedicated crisis lines, secure messaging) in addition to MFA, covering both halves of Art.21(2)(j).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that MFA and secured emergency communication systems are operational.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search verifying emergency communication channel availability and encryption status.

dataSources: UC platform health (`uc_platform:health`), Signal/Teams/Zulip audit logs, out-of-band communication test results
spl:
| inputlookup emergency_comm_channels.csv
| join type=outer channel_id [search index=itsm sourcetype IN ("uc_health", "comms:test_result") earliest=-30d
  | stats latest(status) AS last_status latest(_time) AS last_test_time latest(encryption_verified) AS encrypted BY channel_id]
| eval days_since_test=round((now()-last_test_time)/86400)
| eval channel_ok=if(last_status="operational" AND encrypted="true" AND days_since_test<=30, 1, 0)
| append [search index=auth sourcetype IN ("okta:events", "azure:audit") action="authentication" earliest=-7d
  | where match(app, "(?i)(signal|teams|zulip|mattermost|crisis)")
  | eval mfa_used=if(isnotnull(mfa_method) AND mfa_method!="none", 1, 0)
  | stats count AS auth_events sum(mfa_used) AS with_mfa BY app
  | eval channel_id=app, channel_type="comms_platform", last_status="auth_check", encrypted="n/a", days_since_test=0, channel_ok=if(with_mfa/auth_events>=0.95, 1, 0)]
| where channel_ok=0
| table channel_id, channel_type, last_status, encrypted, days_since_test, channel_ok
description: Verifies that both MFA and secure emergency communication channels are operational per NIS2 Art.21(2)(j). Checks out-of-band communication system availability, encryption status, and test recency alongside MFA enforcement on communication platforms.
value: Closes the gap in Art.21(2)(j) coverage by addressing the 'secured communications' requirement that was previously covered only by MFA monitoring.
implementation: (1) Maintain emergency_comm_channels.csv with all crisis communication systems; (2) Schedule monthly communication channel tests; (3) Verify MFA on all communication platforms; (4) Alert when any channel fails verification.
visualization: Table of emergency communication channels with status. Single value for channels passing verification. Bar chart of MFA coverage by platform.
knownFalsePositives: Operational noise during large maintenance windows, controlled exercises, or scope changes to critical entity lists.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Maintain emergency_comm_channels.csv lookup with all crisis communication channels.
• Configure health checks for out-of-band communication systems.

Step 1 — Register channels
Populate emergency_comm_channels.csv with channel_id, channel_type, expected_encryption.

Step 2 — Create the search
(see SPL above)

Step 3 — Validate
Compare ticket and field counts with the ServiceNow ITSM or GRC console (same time range) and confirm synchronization fields and sourcetypes used by this search.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Schedule daily. Alert on any channel_ok=0.
grandmaExplanation: We verifies that both and secure emergency communication channels are operational per NIS2 Art.21(2)(j).
